chapter_name: The Living Organisms and Their Surroundings

questions.question: Aquatic animals respire by using
questions.explanation: Aquatic animals respire by using dissolved oxygen in water by using gills. Gills are modified lungs for aquatic animals, they take oxygen present in the water and respire.

questions.question: Which of the following is a biotic components
questions.question_html: Which of the following is a biotic components
questions.explanation: Bacteria and fungi are a biotic organisms that act as decomposer. Water, soil and air are abiotic components. Decomposers are responsible for taking out the dead and dying organisms and produce nutrients to the soil.

questions.question: Which of the following cannot be called a habitat?
questions.question_html: Which of the following cannot be called a habitat?

questions.question: Following are some features of plants They lose a lot of water through transpiration. Their leaves are always broad and flat. They lose very little water through transpiration. Their roots grow very deep into the soil. Which of the combination of above features are typical of desert plants?
questions.question_html:
Following are some features of plants

  1. They lose a lot of water through transpiration.
  2. Their leaves are always broad and flat.
  3. They lose very little water through transpiration.
  4. Their roots grow very deep into the soil.

Which of the combination of above features are typical of desert plants?

questions.question: Boojho comes across an animal having a stream-lined and slippery body. What is the habitat of the animal?
questions.question_html: Boojho comes across an animal having a stream-lined and slippery body. What is the habitat of the animal?

questions.question: Organisms that obtain their food from others are called
questions.question_html: Organisms that obtain their food from others are called
questions.explanation: Organism that obtains their food from others are called consumers. An organisms that lacks the ability to produce there own food from inorganic sources and hence obtain food from other organisms are called as consumers.

questions.question: Some animals hibernate to

options.text: Preventing loss of water from body

questions.explanation: Cold winter months, nothing seems more inviting than a warm bed. But for some animals, hunkering down in a cozy den when nights are long and temperatures are low isn't just a matter of temporary comfort — it's necessary for survival.

questions.question: Which one is a abiotic factor of environment
questions.question_html: Which one is a abiotic factor of environment
questions.explanation: Producer, consumer and decomposer are biotic factor while air is abiotic factor of environment. Abiotic factors are nonliving chemical and physical part of the environment which affects the living organism and ecosystem.

questions.question: Shape, size and other features of plants depends upon their
questions.question_html: Shape, size and other features of plants depends upon their
questions.explanation: Shape, size and other features of plants depend upon the surroundings of the organisms. It also depends on various other factors like temperature availability of resources and also on the internal parts of the plants like cell, neuron etc.

questions.question: Living creatures of various kind are called
questions.question_html: Living creatures of various kind are called
questions.explanation: Living creatures of various kinds are called organisms having characteristic features. All types of organisms that are capable of reproduction, growth, development is called an organism. It can be unicellular or multicellular.
